utilityName: City of Prescott Backflow Prevention Program
dueBasis: Prescott says all required backflow devices must be tested at installation and every year after that. The city requires customers to use one of Prescott's approved assembly testers and routes reports back to the Water Protection Office.
coveredPropertyTypes: Other locations where the city requires containment
officialListLabel: Open Prescott's approved backflow tester list
penalties: Prescott keeps required assemblies inside the city's containment program and expects annual testing through approved testers. Missing the installation or annual test leaves the customer outside the accepted utility workflow.
sourceExcerpt: Prescott is a strong Arizona utility because the city publishes installation-plus-annual testing language, irrigation and fire-connection triggers, and a live approved tester PDF from the Water Protection Office.
verdictSummary: Prescott is a strong Northern Arizona utility because the city ties annual testing, approved testers, irrigation triggers, and fire-protection assemblies into one clear utility workflow.
whoIsAffected: Prescott water customers with required containment assemblies, including irrigation, commercial, multifamily, and fire-protection connections.
residentialNotes: Residential demand is strongest when the property has irrigation or another utility-required containment assembly.
Prescott routes users into the city tester list instead of leaving the next step to generic plumbing search.
commercialNotes: Commercial value is strong because Prescott publishes both the approved tester list and utility engineering standards.
Fire-protection and irrigation triggers make this page more actionable than a generic annual-testing article.

annualTesting.summary: Prescott says required devices are tested at installation and annually thereafter using approved testers.
highlights: Initial testing and annual testing are both explicit.
Prescott publishes an approved tester PDF rather than sending users to a generic search.
Reports route back through the Water Protection Office workflow.
workflowSteps: Confirm the device is in Prescott's backflow program.
Choose a tester from the approved Prescott list.
Complete the installation or annual test and keep the city record current.

irrigation.summary: Prescott's engineering standards make irrigation a real utility subpage because irrigation services are one of the common triggers for containment assemblies.
highlights: Irrigation systems are a normal local reason a device enters the annual testing cycle.
The same approved tester path still applies.
Customers should keep irrigation work tied to the city's water-protection rules rather than a generic landscaper workflow.
workflowSteps: Confirm the irrigation connection still matches Prescott's containment rules.
Use a Prescott-approved tester for the annual test.
Keep the result in the city workflow before the season gets away from you.

fireLine.summary: Prescott's standards treat fire-protection connections as part of the backflow containment program rather than as a separate afterthought.
highlights: Fire-protection assemblies are explicitly part of the city's engineering standards.
The city still expects approved testers on the accepted list.
Fire work should stay tied to the utility workflow, not a generic contractor route.
workflowSteps: Confirm the fire-line assembly class Prescott expects.
Use a Prescott-approved tester who can handle the fire assembly.
Keep the report tied to the city's backflow program.
Confirm the device is under Prescott's backflow prevention program.
Choose a tester from the city-approved list.
Complete the installation or annual test.
Keep the utility-side record current with Prescott.
failureHighlights: Prescott says required devices are tested at installation and annually thereafter.
The city publishes an approved tester list instead of leaving users in a generic search path.
Irrigation and fire connections sit inside the same utility containment workflow.

costBand.testingRange: Testing is market-priced, but the city-approved tester gate matters more than a published retail number.
costBand.repairRetestRange: Repair and retest cost depends on the assembly type and whether the issue sits on irrigation or fire service.
costBand.pricingNotes: The real Prescott friction is staying inside the approved-tester and water-protection workflow.
